title: Yandex Dictionary API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yandex Dictionary API key detected. This key provides authenticated access to Yandex Dictionary and Translation services including word definitions, translations, synonyms, and language detection. Unauthorized access can lead to API quota abuse, service billing fraud, and potential data mining of translation requests. Immediately revoke this key from your Yandex Developer console and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yandex API token detected. This token grants access to Yandex Dictionary and translation services with potential extended permissions. Exposing Yandex tokens enables unauthorized API usage, quota theft, and potential abuse of translation services for commercial purposes. Revoke immediately from your Yandex Developer account and implement secure credential storage.

Yandex client ID or application identifier detected. These identifiers are used to authenticate applications with Yandex Dictionary and translation services. While less sensitive than API keys, exposing client IDs can reveal application configuration details and enable targeted attacks against your Yandex integrations. Store securely using environment variables or configuration management.
